public_reputation: Quietly feared because he remembers who promised what.
appearance: Pale ash sleeves, neat witness cords, and ink-dark nails from constant copying.
daily_function: Records witness statements, contract tiers, and special rites for the dead.
current_agenda: Recover one missing witness folio before a disputed burial turns public.
hidden_pressure: A missing folio threatens the assumption that Panim records are final.
public_signal: His inked fingers make grief look archived.
first_impression: Scribe Iren Palemark presents as Writes before answering. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Witness Scribe, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Principality of Panim.
what_they_refuse: Scribe Iren Palemark refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Witness Scribe too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Scribe Iren Palemark can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Scribe Iren Palemark adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Scribe Iren Palemark is Quietly feared because he remembers who promised what. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Witness Scribe.
